'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = {{ill|Shigeoka Kawahito|ja|滋岳川人}} | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 16, 5th month, 27th day ({{ill|874|ja|874年}} {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}) | renamed = | alias = | posthumous_name = | shrine_title = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}・{{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} → {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_points = }}
'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Shigeoka no Kawahito]][[en:draft:Shigeoka no Kawahito]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Shigeoka no Kawahito|Shigeoka no Kawahito]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = {{ill|Shigeoka Kawahito|ja|滋岳川人}} | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 16, 5th month, 27th day ({{ill|874|ja|874年}} {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}) | renamed = | alias = | posthumous_name = | shrine_title = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}・{{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} → {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_points = }}
'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}
== See also ==
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Shigeoka no Kawahito]][[en:draft:Shigeoka no Kawahito]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Shigeoka no Kawahito|Shigeoka no Kawahito]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = {{ill|Shigeoka Kawahito|ja|滋岳川人}} | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 16, 5th month, 27th day ({{ill|874|ja|874年}} {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}) | renamed = | alias = | posthumous_name = | shrine_title = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}・{{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} → {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_points = }}
'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}
== See also ==

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Shigeoka no Kawahito]][[en:draft:Shigeoka no Kawahito]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Shigeoka no Kawahito|Shigeoka no Kawahito]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = {{ill|Shigeoka Kawahito|ja|滋岳川人}} | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 16, 5th month, 27th day ({{ill|874|ja|874年}} {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}) | renamed = | alias = | posthumous_name = | shrine_title = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}・{{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} → {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_points = }}
'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}
== See also ==
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]
== References == {{Reflist|30em}}
== Bibliography == {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Kaori Nasu|ja|那須香織}}|title={{ill|The Rise of Shigeoka Kawahito and the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|滋岳川人の登場と陰陽寮}}|journal={{ill|Journal of Humanities|ja|学習院大学人文科学論集}}|issue=25}} {{Cite book|author={{ill|Takeda Yūyoshi|ja|武田祐吉}}}}

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = {{ill|Shigeoka Kawahito|ja|滋岳川人}} | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 16, 5th month, 27th day ({{ill|874|ja|874年}} {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}) | renamed = | alias = | posthumous_name = | shrine_title = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}・{{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} → {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_points = }}
'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = {{ill|Shigeoka Kawahito|ja|滋岳川人}} | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 16, 5th month, 27th day ({{ill|874|ja|874年}} {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}) | renamed = | alias = | posthumous_name = | shrine_title = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}・{{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} → {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_points = }}
'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|山城国}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}} ({{ill|854|ja|854年}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年}} ({{ill|858|ja|858年}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2年9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859年}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年}} ({{ill|863|ja|863年}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}
