'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }} ({{ill|854|ja|854th year }}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2nd year }} ({{ill|858|ja|858th year }}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year }}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5th year }} ({{ill|863|ja|863rd year }}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3rd year }}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }} ({{ill|854|ja|854th year }}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2nd year }} ({{ill|858|ja|858th year }}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year }}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5th year }} ({{ill|863|ja|863rd year }}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3rd year }}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }} ({{ill|854|ja|854th year }}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2nd year }} ({{ill|858|ja|858th year }}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year }}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5th year }} ({{ill|863|ja|863rd year }}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3rd year }}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }} ({{ill|854|ja|854th year }}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2nd year }} ({{ill|858|ja|858th year }}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year }}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5th year }} ({{ill|863|ja|863rd year }}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3rd year }}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }} ({{ill|854|ja|854th year }}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2nd year }} ({{ill|858|ja|858th year }}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year }}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5th year }} ({{ill|863|ja|863rd year }}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3rd year }}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Shigeoka no Kawahito]][[en:draft:Shigeoka no Kawahito]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Shigeoka no Kawahito|Shigeoka no Kawahito]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = {{ill|Shigeoka Kawahito|ja|滋岳川人}} | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 16, 5th month, 27th day ({{ill|874|ja|874th year }} {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}) | renamed = | alias = | posthumous_name = | shrine_title = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}・{{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}} | clan = {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} → {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_points = }}
'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }} ({{ill|854|ja|854th year }}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2nd year }} ({{ill|858|ja|858th year }}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year }}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5th year }} ({{ill|863|ja|863rd year }}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3rd year }}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}
== See also ==
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]
== References == {{Reflist|30em}}
== Bibliography == {{Cite journal|author={{ill|Kaori Nasu|ja|那須香織}}|title={{ill|The Rise of Shigeoka Kawahito and the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|滋岳川人の登場と陰陽寮}}|journal={{ill|Journal of Humanities|ja|学習院大学人文科学論集}}|issue=25}} {{Cite book|author={{ill|Takeda Yūyoshi|ja|武田祐吉}}}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }} ({{ill|854|ja|854th year }}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2nd year }} ({{ill|858|ja|858th year }}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year }}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5th year }} ({{ill|863|ja|863rd year }}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3rd year }}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }} ({{ill|854|ja|854th year }}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2nd year }} ({{ill|858|ja|858th year }}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year }}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5th year }} ({{ill|863|ja|863rd year }}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3rd year }}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }} ({{ill|854|ja|854th year }}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2nd year }} ({{ill|858|ja|858th year }}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year }}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5th year }} ({{ill|863|ja|863rd year }}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3rd year }}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = {{ill|Shigeoka Kawahito|ja|滋岳川人}} | image = | caption = | era = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 16, 5th month, 27th day ({{ill|874|ja|874th year }} {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}}) | renamed = | alias = | posthumous_name = | shrine_title = | religious_name = | burial_place = | rank = {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}・{{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} → {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}} | clan = {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} → {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}} | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_points = }}
'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. His court rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}.
== Lineage == The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District|ja|美方郡}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen Town|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造}} lineage.
== Career == In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }} ({{ill|854|ja|854th year }}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣}}. At the time, he held the rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented: - In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2nd year }} ({{ill|858|ja|858th year }}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|ja|文徳天皇}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province }}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年}} ({{ill|859|ja|859th year }}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District|ja|吉野郡}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref> - In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5th year }} ({{ill|863|ja|863rd year }}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref> Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takamayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}.
== Works and Legacy == Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.
== Timeline of Ranks == * {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4th year }}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3rd year }}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 滋岳川人 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[平安時代]]前期 | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]16年[[5月27日 (旧暦)|5月27日]]（[[874年]][[7月14日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従五位|従五位上]]・[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]] | 主君 = [[文徳天皇]]→[[清和天皇]] | 氏族 = 刀岐[[直 (姓)|直]]→滋岳[[朝臣]] | 父母 = | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''滋岳 川人'''（しげおか の かわひと）は、[[平安時代]]前期の[[貴族]]・[[陰陽家]]。[[氏姓制度|氏姓]]は刀岐[[直 (姓)|直]]のち滋岳[[朝臣]]。[[官位]]は[[従五位|従五位上]]・[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]]。
== 出自 == 刀岐氏（刀岐直）の出自は明らかでないが、[[但馬国]][[二方郡]]刀岐郷（現在の[[兵庫県]][[美方郡]][[新温泉町]]）に由来する一族と考えられる{{sfn|太田|1963|loc=3908}}。また直姓であることから、二方国造の一族とも考えられる。
== 経歴 == [[仁寿]]4年（[[854年]]）一族の[[上総国#国司|上総少目]]・刀岐雄貞らと同時に刀岐直から滋岳朝臣に改姓する（この時の[[官位]]は[[正六位|正六位上]]・[[陰陽寮|陰陽権允]]兼[[陰陽博士]]）。その後も[[文徳天皇|文徳朝]]から[[清和天皇|清和朝]]にかけて20年以上の長きに亘って陰陽博士として[[陰陽道]]をもって[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]に仕え、[[天安 (日本)|天安]]元年（[[857年]]）陰陽権助、のち陰陽頭にまで昇った。陰陽家として、以下の活動が記録に残っている。 *天安2年（[[858年]]）[[大納言]]・[[安倍安仁]]らと共に、[[山城国]][[葛野郡]]田邑郷真原岡（現在の[[京都市]][[右京区]]太秦三尾町付近）を文徳天皇陵の地に定めた<ref>『日本三代実録』天安2年9月2日条</ref>。 * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）[[備後国#国司|備後介]]・[[藤原山蔭]]と共に[[大和国]][[吉野郡]]高山に派遣され、[[五穀]]への虫害を避けるために[[董仲舒]]の祭法を用いて祭礼を修した<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>。 *貞観5年（[[863年]]）[[陰陽師]]を率いて大和国吉野郡高山に赴き、虫害を避けるために祭礼を修した<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5年2月1日条</ref>。 こののち、[[旱魃]]と[[飢饉]]が発生した貞観8年（[[866年]]）にも高山祭への遣使が行われているが<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観8年7月2日条</ref>、この時にも陰陽博士を務めていた川人が高山祭に奉仕していた可能性が高く、高山祭成立期における川人の存在の大きさが窺われる{{sfn|那須香織|2016|p=46}}。 この間、斉衡2年（[[855年]]）[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）従五位下に叙される。のち、貞観3年（[[862年]]）[[播磨国#国司|播磨権大掾]]、貞観7年（[[865年]]）播磨権介、貞観16年（[[874年]]）[[安芸国#国司|安芸権介]]と清和朝では地方官も兼帯した。
貞観16年（[[874年]]）5月27日卒去。最終官位は従五位上陰陽頭兼陰陽博士安芸権介。
== 人物 == [[平安時代]]初期までの[[陰陽寮]]では中国由来の[[漢籍]]に基づき技術の習得や職務の遂行が行われていたところ、9世紀後半になると日本独自の陰陽道書籍が現れるようになったが、その中でも滋岳川人は多くの著作を残した先駆けの存在として知られている。『世要動静経』『指掌宿曜経』『滋川新術遁甲書』『金匱新注』などの著書があったとされ<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観16年5月27日条</ref>、いずれも散逸し現存しないが、後代の陰陽道関係の典籍には、川人の著作からの引用がしばしばみられ、以降の陰陽道へ与えた影響は大きい{{sfn|那須香織|2016|p=035}}。9世紀後半を日本独自の陰陽道の成立期とみる山下克明は川人を「陰陽道成立の流れを切り拓いた人物」であるとして、陰陽道の歴史における川人の役割を評価している<ref>山下克明『陰陽道 <small>術数と信仰の文化</small>』【王朝時代の実像 5】（臨川出版、2022年）ISBN 978-4-653-04705-6 P66-67.</ref>。
また、[[平安時代]]末期に成立した『[[今昔物語集]]』では、[[文徳天皇]]陵の設置地の選定が発端となり[[天津神・国津神|地神]]の追跡をうけるも、呪を唱えて逃れたとの逸話があることから<ref>『今昔物語集』巻第24,第13「滋岳川人、地神に追はれし語」</ref>、後世にわたり有名な[[陰陽師]]として知られたことが窺われる。
== 官歴 == 『[[六国史]]』による。 * 時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]]。[[陰陽寮|陰陽権允]]兼[[陰陽博士]] * [[仁寿]]4年（[[854年]]） 9月5日：刀岐直から滋岳朝臣に改姓 * [[斉衡]]2年（[[855年]]） 正月7日：[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]] * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]元年（[[857年]]） 12月9日：陰陽権助、陰陽博士如元 * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]） 11月19日：従五位下（[[内位]]） * 貞観3年（[[862年]]） 正月13日：兼[[播磨国#国司|播磨権大掾]] * 貞観7年（[[865年]]） 正月27日：兼播磨権介 * 貞観8年（[[866年]]） 12月29日：[[侍従|次侍従]] * 時期不詳：従五位上。[[陰陽頭]] * 貞観16年（[[874年]]） 正月15日：兼[[安芸国#国司|安芸権介]]。5月27日：卒去（従五位上行陰陽頭兼陰陽博士安芸権介）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|30em}}
== 参考文献 == {{参照方法|date=2023年11月|section=1}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=那須香織 |date=2016-10 |url=https://glim-re.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/5106 |title=滋岳川人の登場と九世紀の陰陽寮 |journal=学習院大学人文科学論集 |ISSN=09190791 |publisher=学習院大学大学院人文科学研究科 |issue=25 |pages=35-60 |hdl=10959/00005095 |ref=harv}} * [[武田祐吉]]、[[佐藤謙三]]訳『読み下し 日本三代実録 上巻』[[戎光祥出版]]、2009年 * {{cite book|和書|author=村山修一 |title=日本陰陽道史総説 |publisher=塙書房 |year=1981 |ISBN=4827310572 |NCID=BN01612992}} * [[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年 * {{Cite book|和書|author=太田亮 |author-link=太田亮 |title=姓氏家系大辞典 |publisher=角川書店 |year=1963 |ref={{harvid|太田|1963}}}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:しけおか の かわひと}} [[Category:平安時代前期の貴族]] [[Category:陰陽師]] [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:874年没]]

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|qq=|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|qq=|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|qq=|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==

* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|qq=|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|qq=|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|qq=|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|qq=|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|qq=|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
