'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] Togi area in [[:en:Tajima Province|Tajima Province]] (modern [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen Town]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:854|854]]), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:858|858]]), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for [[:en:Emperor Montoku|Emperor Montoku]] in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:859|859]]), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] to prevent pestilence.<ref>『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:863|863]]), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takamayama Festival]].

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District, Hyōgo|en|Mikata District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1155035|lt=Mikata District}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|WD=Q1053727|lt=Tajima Province}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|en|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|WD=Q1154955|lt=Shin'onsen Town}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|854|en|854|WD=Q24981|lt=854}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|858|en|858|WD=Q30371|lt=858}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|859|en|859|WD=Q23897|lt=859}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|en|Yoshino District, Nara|WD=Q1154558|lt=Yoshino District}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takamayama Festival}}.

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

'''Shigeoka Kawahito''' (滋岳川人) was a {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} aristocrat and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}}. His family name transitioned from {{ill|Togi no Ason|ja|刀岐直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togi no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His court Rank reached {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and he served as the {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}.

== Lineage ==
The origins of the Togi clan (刀岐氏) are unclear, but they are thought to be descended from the {{ill|Mikata District, Hyōgo|en|Mikata District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1155035|lt=Mikata District}} Togi area in {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|WD=Q1053727|lt=Tajima Province}} (modern {{ill|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|en|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|WD=Q1154955|lt=Shin'onsen Town}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}){{sfn|Ōta|1963|loc=3908}}. As a clan with the "Na" surname (直), they may have also been part of the {{ill|Mikata Province Governors|ja|二方国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikata Province Governors|1=Futakata Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487217}} lineage.

== Career ==
In {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|854|en|854|WD=Q24981|lt=854}}), Kawahito and his relatives, including Togi no Masaru (then serving as {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}} Provincial Secretary), were granted the surname {{ill|Shigeoka no Ason|ja|滋岳朝臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shigeoka no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. At the time, he held the Rank {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} and the positions {{ill|Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽権允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}. Kawahito continued to serve as an Onmyō Doctor for more than 20 years, spanning the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku}} and {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, eventually ascending to {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. As an Onmyōji, his activities are well documented:
- In {{ill|Tian'an 2|ja|天安2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tian'an 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|858|en|858|WD=Q30371|lt=858}}), Kawahito, along with {{ill|Abe no Asomi|ja|安倍安仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Asomi|1=Abe no Yasuhito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450312}} and others, selected a burial site for {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku}} in the {{ill|Katsuno District|ja|葛野郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno District|1=Kadono district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13579879}}, {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』天安2nd year 9月2日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 1|ja|貞観元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|859|en|859|WD=Q23897|lt=859}}), he and {{ill|Fujihara no Yamakage|ja|藤原山蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujihara no Yamakage|1=Fujiwara no Yamakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623214}} performed a ritual in {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|en|Yoshino District, Nara|WD=Q1154558|lt=Yoshino District}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} to prevent pestilence.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』貞観元年8月3日条</ref>
- In {{ill|Jōgan 5|ja|貞観5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863}}), he conducted further rituals to address similar concerns.<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観5th year 2月1日条</ref>
Kawahito's activities significantly influenced the rituals and practices during this period, particularly in the establishment of the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takamayama Festival}}.

== Works and Legacy ==
Kawahito is credited with foundational works in Japanese {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}}, including 『{{ill|Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja|世要動静経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiyō Dōsei-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』 and 『{{ill|Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja|滋川新術遁甲書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinshū Shinjutsu Tonkō-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}貞観16年5月27日条</ref> Although these are lost, they heavily influenced subsequent practitioners and texts.

== Timeline of Ranks ==
* {{ill|Ninju 4|ja|仁寿4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}
* {{ill|Jōgan 3|ja|貞観3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Appointed {{ill|Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja|播磨権大掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Assistant Governor of Harima Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
